Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8 PM

Noise Complaints Presents: The Late Night R&B Experience Tour What started as a YouTube series has now become a nationwide movement. Now we're bringing it live to a city near you. item.title: Hatebreed: SUMMER SLAUGHTER TOUR 2026
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: House of Blues Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

BOX OFFICE: 3:00 DOORS: 4:00 All Ages Welcome We're cashless! All points of sale will only accept credit, debit or mobile pay (such as Apple Pay or Google Pay). Will call tickets can be picked up on day of show at the Box Office. Box Office opens 1 hour prior to door times. You will need a valid photo ID and the credit card used for purchase. ADA tickets can be purchased online - you do not need to call the box office to place an order. Look for the logo on the Find Tickets page to view all available accessible seats. We do have accessible seating in every price range but be advised it may sell out. We have very limited seating available for guests on the day of the event so please make sure you purchase accessible seating when you order tickets. Bags up to 12" x 6" x 12" are allowed in the venue. All bags will be searched prior to entry. For more FAQs please visit www.houston.houseofblues.com/faq BOX OFFICE: 3:00 DOORS: 4:00 All Ages Welcome We're cashless! item.title: Cynthia Lee Fontaine Presents: Drag Brunch at the Punch
item.description:
Saturday, July 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Punch Line Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Looking for the ultimate Houston drag brunch experience? Whether you're planning a bachelorette party, a birthday celebration, or just want a daytime drag show without the late-night crowd, this is your event. Drag Brunch at The Punch is a monthly extravaganza featuring fierce performances, sassy audience games, and unforgettable live drag performances ? paired with brunch cocktails, mimosas, and bites. The perfect daytime LGBTQ event for anyone who loves fabulous entertainment before sundown. Produced by RuPaul's Drag Race fan-favorite Cynthia Lee Fontaine, each show features Houston drag queens bringing show-stopping numbers, big laughs, and serious talent. Perfect for girls' day out, gay brunch, Pride celebrations, and anyone looking for a fun Saturday activity in Houston. No late nights required ? just good drinks, great queens, and the best drag brunch ticket in town. Grab your tickets now. Please note that Cynthia will not be making an appearance. IMPORTANT TIMES: 10:30 AM BOX OFFICE OPENS SEATING BEGINS AT 11:00AM - SHOWTIME IS AT 12:00PM This show is 18 & over with valid, government-issued photo ID. Cameras and video/audio recording devices are not permitted. $.25 per each ticket sold for a majority of events at Punch Line benefits Comedy Gives Back, a safety net for the comedy community. For more information, please visit www.comedygivesback.com Our events often run for multiple dates/times. item.title: The Marvelous Miss Gender starring BOSCO - Ages 18+ w/ Valid ID
item.description:
Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: House of Blues Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. From RuPaul's Drag Race Season 14, 2022 and most recently All Stars 10 from 2025, BOSCO is now headlining her first ever solo tour. Meet & Greet add-on tickets are sold separately. To attend the M&G, fans must also purchase a ticket to the event. Show starts at 8pm. There will be a 20-minute intermission. Ages 18+ welcome w/ Valid ID for entry. Check DragFans.com and HereIsBosco.com for tour info VIP Tables seat 4 humans and are first come first serve if you purchase in that section. Please arrive early so that our hosts may seat your party together. Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. item.title: Cynthia Lee Fontaine Presents: Drag Brunch at the Punch
item.description:
Saturday, August 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Punch Line Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Looking for the ultimate Houston drag brunch experience? Whether you're planning a bachelorette party, a birthday celebration, or just want a daytime drag show without the late-night crowd, this is your event. Drag Brunch at The Punch is a monthly extravaganza featuring fierce performances, sassy audience games, and unforgettable live drag performances ? paired with brunch cocktails, mimosas, and bites. The perfect daytime LGBTQ event for anyone who loves fabulous entertainment before sundown. Produced by RuPaul's Drag Race fan-favorite Cynthia Lee Fontaine, each show features Houston drag queens bringing show-stopping numbers, big laughs, and serious talent. Perfect for girls' day out, gay brunch, Pride celebrations, and anyone looking for a fun Saturday activity in Houston. No late nights required ? just good drinks, great queens, and the best drag brunch ticket in town. Grab your tickets now. Please note that Cynthia will not be making an appearance. IMPORTANT TIMES: 10:30 AM BOX OFFICE OPENS SEATING BEGINS AT 11:00AM - SHOWTIME IS AT 12:00PM This show is 18 & over with valid, government-issued photo ID. Cameras and video/audio recording devices are not permitted. $.25 per each ticket sold for a majority of events at Punch Line benefits Comedy Gives Back, a safety net for the comedy community. For more information, please visit www.comedygivesback.com Our events often run for multiple dates/times. item.title: The Music Man
item.description:
Tuesday, September 15, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: The hobby center, 800 Bagby St, Houston, TX 77002, United States, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Seventy-six trombones. One unforgettable musical! Meredith Willson’s six-time Tony Award®-winning beloved musical comedy The Music Man marches into Houston with laughter, heart, and all the music you love. Following the fast-talking traveling salesman Harold Hill as he cons the people of River City, Iowa, into buying instruments and uniforms for a boys’ band that he vows to organize. Featuring songs such as “Seventy-Six Trombones,” “Trouble,” “’Till There Was You,” “Pick-a-Little,” and “Gary, Indiana,” The Music Man is a classic musical to be shared with every generation. CRITICAL ACCLAIM FOR THE MUSIC MAN – The Telegraph “A joyous, blissful Broadway event!” – Variety “Grand and Glorious!” “…totally enchanting abso-tootin’-lutely smashing revival. It will give you new life.” PERFORMANCE SCHEDULE Tuesday, Sept 15, 7:30 p.m. – H-E-B Community Night Wednesday, Sept 16, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 17, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 18, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 2 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 2 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 7:30 p.m. Tuesday, Sept 22, 7:30 p.m. Wednesday, Sept 23, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 24, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 25, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 26, 2 p.m. — Open Captioned Saturday, Sept 26, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 27, 2 p.m. — ASL & Audio Described SUBSCRIBE NOW TICKETING INFO Season tickets are now available. 6-show packages start at just $34 per-show. Tickets will start at $46 and will be available beginning July 13 online or by calling TUTS Guest Services at 713-558-8887. Tickets will also available in person Monday through Friday from Noon to 6 p.m. at the TUTS Box Office, located in the Hobby Center lobby. Discounted group tickets are available for parties of 10 or more.Theatre Under The Stars has a variety of accessibility options for guests.Learn more about accessible performances and other services here. Rush tickets will be available for $35 at the box office for mezzanine and gallery seating starting 2 hours prior to curtain. Questions about parking, dining or other aspects of attending a TUTS show? We’ve got answers! RUN TIME 2 hours and 30 minutes including intermission Photos Photos by Marshall Meadows VIDEOS CREATIVE TEAM Matt Lenz Director Joshua Bergasse Choreographer Ann Beyersdorfer Set Designer Santo Loquasto Original Broadway and Tour Costume Designer Ken Billington Lighting Designer Lisa Renkel Video & Projection Designer Walter Trarbach Sound Designer Roxanne De Luna Wig & Makeup Designer Robin L. McGee Costume Coordinator Kristin Stowell Music Supervisor Thomas Fosnocht Music Director/Orchestrator ARC Casting Dan Sher/Big League Productions, Inc Producer BEHIND THE SCENES ELLIOTT ANDREWS as “Harold Hill” ELIZABETH D’AIUTO as “Marian Paroo” CHARLES ANTENEN as “Tommy Djilas” PATRICK BLASHILL as “Mayor Shinn” ADDIE JAYMES as “Amaryllis” DYLAN PATTERSON as “Winthrop Paroo” MICHAEL SANTORA as “Charlie Cowell” MADISON SHEWARD as “Zaneeta Shinn” SAVANNAH STEVENSON as “Mrs. Paroo” PAUL URRIOLA as “Marcellus Washburn” EMMANUELLE ZEESMAN as “Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” CHRISTIAN ANDREWS as “Conductor, Constable Locke, Ensemble, u/s Olin Britt” BRYCE BAYER as “Dance Ensemble Swing, Assistant Dance Captain” ANNA CHIN as “Dance Ensemble” DANNY CRUZ as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” KALISTA CURBELO as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Zaneeta Shinn” WHITNEY DANIELS as “Alma Hix, u/s Marian Paroo” ERIN DIEHL as “Dance Ensemble, Dance Captain” CRAIG FIRST as “Jacey Squires, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Charlie Cowell” BRIAN DOOLITTLE GONZÁLEZ as “Ewart Dunlop, u/s Charlie Cowell, u/s Harold Hill” SOPHIE GORON as “Swing” ALEX ISHIDA as “Ethel Toffelmeir, u/s Marian Paroo” CAMERON JANSON as “Dance Ensemble” HANNAH ROSE KIDWELL as “Mrs. Squires, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” KEVIN KUSKA as “Swing, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Mayor Shinn” ROSE MESSENGER as “Maud Dunlop, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” JOSHUA PIERRE MOORE as “Olin Britt, u/s Mayor Shinn” KYLIE NOELLE PATTERSON as “Child Onstage Swing – Amaryllis/Winthrop” BRANDON RANALLI as “Dance Ensemble” AIDAN RAWLINSON as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” SHAE REYNOLDS as “Gracie Shinn, Dance Ensemble” BRAYDEN SCHILLING as “Traveling Salesperson, Ensemble, u/s Harold Hill, u/s Barbershop Quartet” MARIELLE UTAYDE as “Dance Ensemble Swing, u/s Zaneeta Shinn, u/s Gracie Shinn” MATTHEW WAUTIER-RODRIGUEZ as “Oliver Hix”

item.title: Cynthia Lee Fontaine Presents: Drag Brunch at the Punch
item.description:
Saturday, September 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Punch Line Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Looking for the ultimate Houston drag brunch experience? Whether you're planning a bachelorette party, a birthday celebration, or just want a daytime drag show without the late-night crowd, this is your event. Drag Brunch at The Punch is a monthly extravaganza featuring fierce performances, sassy audience games, and unforgettable live drag performances ? paired with brunch cocktails, mimosas, and bites. The perfect daytime LGBTQ event for anyone who loves fabulous entertainment before sundown. Produced by RuPaul's Drag Race fan-favorite Cynthia Lee Fontaine, each show features Houston drag queens bringing show-stopping numbers, big laughs, and serious talent. Perfect for girls' day out, gay brunch, Pride celebrations, and anyone looking for a fun Saturday activity in Houston. No late nights required ? just good drinks, great queens, and the best drag brunch ticket in town. Grab your tickets now. Please note that Cynthia will not be making an appearance. IMPORTANT TIMES: 10:30 AM BOX OFFICE OPENS SEATING BEGINS AT 11:00AM - SHOWTIME IS AT 12:00PM This show is 18 & over with valid, government-issued photo ID. item.description:
Saturday, September 26, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 713 Music Hall, 401 Franklin Street, Houston, TX

Doors: 6:30 PM Show: 8:00 PM Ages: All Ages **All support acts are subject to change without notice** No Resale Over Face Value: Freya Skye is using Ticketmaster's Face Value Exchange to help fans get tickets at the original price. Tickets can only be resold on Ticketmaster at face value. States Blocking Artist Resale Terms: New York, Illinois, Colorado, Virginia, Utah and Connecticut have state laws that prevent resale restrictions. In those states, Ticketmaster will still honor Freya Skye's terms by keeping resale prices at face value on its site. To reduce staff contact with guest belongings, we have implemented the following bag policy: Bags up to 12" x 6" x 12" are allowed in the venue. All bags will be searched prior to entry. Bags that are not clear will be subject to additional search. Doors: 6:30 PM Show: 8:00 PM Ages: All Ages **All support acts are subject to change without notice** To reduce staff contact with guest belongings, we have implemented the following bag policy: Bags up to 12" x 6" x 12" are allowed in the venue. All bags will be searched prior to entry. Bags that are not clear will be subject to additional search. Freya Skye is contributing $1 per ticket sold to PLUS1, a 501(c)(3), to benefit Active Minds in mobilizing youth and young adults to lead a transformative movement in mental health, forever changing how it is valued and prioritized in society. No portion of the ticket purchase is tax deductible. Please see www.plus1.org for more information
